risks: No availability zones configured on either VMSS or SQL MI — limits resilience to datacenter-level failures
No NSG resources defined for any subnet — Standard Load Balancer blocks all inbound traffic by default without NSGs, and WAF requires NSGs on all subnets
No monitoring or diagnostics resources (Log Analytics, Application Insights, Azure Monitor) included in the plan
SQL MI subnet uses /27 (minimum 32 addresses) — leaves no room for scaling or adding instances; in-place subnet expansion is not supported
No Key Vault resource for managing TDE customer-managed encryption keys (plan relies on service-managed keys)
Application tier VMSS has autoscale disabled — may become a bottleneck under variable load
correctionsRecommended: Add availability zone configuration (zones property) to both VMSS resources and consider enabling zoneRedundant on SQL MI
Add NSG resources for each subnet with appropriate inbound/outbound rules — required for Standard LB inbound connectivity
Add an Azure Monitor Log Analytics workspace and diagnostic settings for operational visibility
Expand SQL MI subnet to /26 or larger to accommodate future growth and management operations
Consider enabling autoscale on the application tier VMSS to handle variable load
Add an explicit Public IP resource (Standard SKU) for the presentation load balancer
Consider replacing the Layer-4 Load Balancer with Application Gateway (with WAF) for the internet-facing presentation tier to gain Layer-7 routing and web application firewall protection
